elements.sent-2411-001: A graph is $2$ leaves-free if it has no two pendent vertices.
elements.sent-2411-002: In this paper, we give sharp upper bounds on the $Q$-index of 2 leaves-free graphs with given size and characterize the corresponding extremal graphs.
elements.sent-2411-003: Let $G$ be an undirected simple graph with vertex set $V(G)=\{v_{1 ,\cdots, v_{n}\}$ and edge set $E(G)$, where $e(G)$ is the number of edges of $G$.}
elements.sent-2411-004: For $v\in V(G)$, the neighborhood $N_{G (v)$ of $v$ is $\{u: uv\in E(G)\}$ and the degree $d_{G (v)$ of $v$ is $N_{G (u)$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-005: Denote by $\Delta(G)$ and $\delta(G)$ the maximum and minimum degree of $G$, respectively.
elements.sent-2411-006: Denote by $P_{n , C_{n}$, $S_{n}$ the path, the cycle and the star graph of order $n$, respectively.}
elements.sent-2411-007: Denote by $D_{a,b}$ the double star graph consisting of connecting two center vertices of $K_{1,a}$ and $K_{1,b}$.
elements.sent-2411-008: For $A, B \subseteq V(G)$, $e(A)$ denotes the number of the edges of $G$ with both endvertices in $A$ and $e(A, B)$ denotes the number of the edges of $G$ with one endvertex in $A$ and the other in $B$.
elements.sent-2411-009: For two vertex disjoint graphs $G$ and $H$, we denote by $G$ and $H$ and $G\vee H$ the union of $G$ and $H$, and the join of $G$ and $H$, i.e., joining every vertex of $G$ to every vertex of $H$, respectively.
elements.sent-2411-010: Denote by $kG$ the union of $k$ disjoint copies of $G$.
elements.sent-2411-011: Let $S_{n ^{+}$ be the graph obtained from $K_{1,n-1}$ by adding one edge within its independent sets.}
elements.sent-2411-012: For any nonisolated vertex $v$ of $G$, the average degree of $v$ is denoted by $m(v)=\frac{1 {d(v)}\sum_{u\in N(v) d(u)$.}}
elements.sent-2411-013: For graph notation and terminology undefined here,we refer the readers to \cite{BoMu1 .}
elements.sent-2411-014: For a graph $G$, let $A(G)$ denote its adjacency matrix and $D(G)$ denote the diagonal matrix of vertex degrees.
elements.sent-2411-015: The matrix $Q(G)=D(G) + A(G)$ is called the signless Laplacian matrix (or the $Q$-matrix) of $G$.
elements.sent-2411-016: The largest eigenvalues of $A(G)$ and $Q(G)$ are called the index (denoted by $\rho(G)$) and the $Q$-index (denoted by $q(G)$) of $G$, respectively.
elements.sent-2411-017: By the well-known Perron--Frobenius theorem, if $G$ is connected, then $Q(G)$ is irreducible and there exists a unique positive unit eigenvector $X = (x_{1 , x_{2 , \cdots, x_{n )^{T}$ corresponding to $q(G)$ such that $q(G)=X^{\prime QX=\sum_{ij\in E(G) (x_{i +x_{j )^{2}$.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-018: The investigation on the upper or lower bounds of the index and the $Q$-index of graphs is an important and classic topic in the theory of graph spectra.
elements.sent-2411-019: For example, one may see \cite{FSi,LiLF,Ni5,Ni7,Ni3 and the references therein.}
elements.sent-2411-020: Specially, the problem of characterizing the graph with maximal index with given size was posed by Brualdi and Hoffman \cite{BrH as a conjecture, and solved by Rowlinson \cite{Row .}}
elements.sent-2411-021: Motivated by this problem, a good deal of attention has been devoted to determining the graphs with maximum index ($Q$-index) among a given class of graphs with given size.
elements.sent-2411-022: See, for example, \cite{GuoZ,LouG,ZhXL1,ZhXL2 and the references therein.}
elements.sent-2411-023: Zhai, Xue and Luo \cite{ZhXL2 determined the maximal $Q$-index of graphs with given size and clique number (resp., chromatic number).}
elements.sent-2411-024: Zhai, Xue and Liu \cite{ZhXL1 determined the maximal $Q$-index of graphs with given size and matching number.}
elements.sent-2411-025: Lou, Guo and Wang \cite{LouG characterized the unique graph of size $m$ with diameter at least $d$ having the largest $Q$-index and $L$-index.}
elements.sent-2411-026: Jia, Li and Wang \cite{JiaLW ordered all the graphs with given size $m$ and diameter $d$ from the second to the ($\lfloor\frac{d {2}\rfloor+1$)th via their largest $Q$-indices.}}
elements.sent-2411-027: It is worth noting that all those extremal graphs shown in \cite{LouG,ZhXL1,ZhXL2 contain pendent vertices and recently Wang \cite{Wsj determined the maximal $Q$-index of leaf-free graphs with given size and characterized the corresponding extremal graphs completely, which motivates us to consider those graphs with no 2 pendent vertices, that is, the 2 leaves-free graphs.}}
elements.sent-2411-028: Let $G$ be a 2 leaves-free graph with size $m\geq17$.
elements.sent-2411-029: In this section, we introduce some lemmas and notations.
elements.sent-2411-030: For a graph $G$ and a subset $S\subseteq V(G)$, let $G[S]$ be the subgraph of $G$ induced by $S$.
elements.sent-2411-031: For two vertex subsets $S$ and $T$ of $V(G)$ (where $S\cap T$ may not be empty), let $e(S,T)$ denote the number of edges with one endpoint in $S$ and the other in $T$.
elements.sent-2411-032: For a vertex $u\in V(G)$, let $N(u)$ be the neighborhood of the vertex $u$ of $G$, $N^{2 (u)$ be the set of vertices of distance two to $u$.}
